purpose: This repository contains end-to-end tests for Cross River Bank's loan selling system, simulating the entire lending-selling environment to test loan origination, seasoning, interest calculation, fee collection, and loan purchase workflows in a controlled environment.

apis.purpose: Get account information for loan actions
Get accounts for loan type changes
Control transfer delays in the COS mock service

dataEntities.name: Loan
dataEntities.description: Core loan entity with funding details, status, and purchase information

dataEntities.name: Contract
dataEntities.description: Defines relationship between MPL, bank, and investor with fee structures

dataEntities.name: LoanAction
dataEntities.description: Represents actions performed on loans like purchase or interest accrual

dataEntities.name: Transfer
dataEntities.description: Financial transfer between accounts for loan operations

dataEntities.name: Batch
dataEntities.description: Group of loans processed together for purchase

dataEntities.name: AccountConfig
dataEntities.description: Configuration for different account types used in loan transactions

dataEntities.name: TrueUpVolumeFee
dataEntities.description: Volume fee adjustments based on contract terms

messagingPatterns.name: Loan Sale Status Changed
messagingPatterns.description: Publishes events when loan sale status changes

messagingPatterns.name: Batch Purchase Completed
messagingPatterns.description: Publishes events when batch purchase is completed

messagingPatterns.name: True Up Volume Fee Charged
messagingPatterns.description: Publishes events when true-up volume fees are charged

messagingPatterns.name: Investor Changed
messagingPatterns.description: Publishes events when loan investor changes

messagingPatterns.name: Loan Type Changed
messagingPatterns.description: Publishes events when loan type changes

messagingPatterns.name: Transfer Outbox
messagingPatterns.description: Ensures reliable delivery of transfer messages

findings.title: Excessive use of sleep() for synchronization
findings.description: The codebase uses numerous sleep() calls to wait for asynchronous operations to complete. This creates brittle tests that may fail intermittently or take longer than necessary to run. Replace with proper synchronization mechanisms like polling with timeouts or event listeners.
